metadata.name: 7.A.1 Electricity generated from renewable sources
metadata.priority: Reducing the impact of energy on the environment and increasing energy efficiency
metadata.definition: Ratio of  the sum of electricity production from power stations: hydroelectric power station, wind power stations, and electricity produced from biomass, biogas, biofuels (including their co-firing) to total electricity production.
metadata.methodology: Energy from renewable sources is the energy derived from natural, repetitive environmental processes, obtained from renewable non-fossil energy sources (energy: water, wind, solar heat, geothermal heat, waves, sea currents and tides and energy obtained from biomass, biogas, biofuels). In domestic conditions, energy from renewable sources comprises energy obtained from direct use of solar heat, wind, geothermal resources (from the Earth), water and energy generated from biomass, biogas, biofuels.Electricity gross generation - energy transformations output in the form of electrical current, as measured on generator contacts.

metadata.name: 7.A.2 Ratio of electricity production to its consumption
metadata.priority: Reducing the impact of energy on the environment and increasing energy efficiency
metadata.definition: Ratio of electricity production volume to its consumption.
metadata.methodology: Electricity gross generation - energy transformations output in the form of electrical current, as measured on generator contacts.Electricity consumption is the amount of electricity delivered to domestic electricity market including: production (indigenous production) of the energy for own-account and electricity import, reduced by export and balance of domestic stocks.

metadata.name: 7.B.1 Total consumption of electricity per capita
metadata.priority: Ensuring energy security of the region
metadata.definition: Total electricity consumption in relation to number of inhabitants.
metadata.methodology: Electricity consumption is the amount of electricity delivered to domestic electricity market including: production (indigenous production) of the energy for own-account and electricity import, reduced by export and balance of domestic stocks.

metadata.name: 7.B.2 Percentage of population connected to gas supply system
metadata.priority: Ensuring energy security of the region
metadata.definition: Population of households using the gas supply system in terms of total population.
metadata.methodology: The gas network  is a system of  conduits providing gas fuels to consumers by companies operating in the field of gas transmission and distribution. The wiring system distinguishes between: • transmission and distribution network (for high-methane and nitrogen-rich gas) – street cables intended to supply gas to buildings or other facilities via connections, • connections – a system of wires connecting the distribution network with buildings and other facilities.  Data on gas users concern the population in dwellings equipped with gas installations from the grid.  An apartment equipped with gas supply from the network is considered to be an apartment in which there are installations (including reception devices located in the apartment) supplying gas from active connections.  Among the users of gas there are: - using gas on the basis of a contract - households consuming gas on the basis of an individual contract with an energy company, - using gas without a contract - households consuming gas without a contract with an energy company (collective agreements concluded between a cooperative or community and an energy company). Data on the number of gaseous fuel customers come from entities holding a gas sales license and are based on the number of contracts signed with gas customers from the network. Data as of 31 December.

metadata.name: 7.B.3 The density of heating transmission and distribution network per 1 km²
metadata.priority: Ensuring energy security of the region
metadata.definition: The sum of the transmission (main) thermic-line and distribution thermic-line per 1 km² of total area.
metadata.methodology: Transmission (main) thermic-line - system of pipes supplying heating agent to distribution pipes. Distribution thermic-line - system of distribution pipes supplying heating factor to household connections.

metadata.name: 7.B.4 Ratio of electricity production to its consumption
metadata.priority: Ensuring energy security of the region
metadata.definition: Ratio of electricity production volume to its consumption.
metadata.methodology: Electricity gross generation - energy transformations output in the form of electrical current, as measured on generator contacts.Electricity consumption is the amount of electricity delivered to domestic electricity market including: production (indigenous production) of the energy for own-account and electricity import, reduced by export and balance of domestic stocks.

metadata.name: 7.C.1 Share of electricity consumers in the total number of inhabitants
metadata.priority: Modernization and expansion of energy infrastructure
metadata.definition: Number of electricity consumers in households per population.
metadata.methodology: Consumers of electric energy in households are final customers purchasing electric energy exclusively for household uses. Information on the number of electricity consumers concerns households and collective households with comprehensive contracts and distribution service contracts. Data as of 31 December.

metadata.name: 7.C.2 Share of gaseous pollution retained or neutralized in pollutant reduction systems in the total pollutants generated
metadata.priority: Modernization and expansion of energy infrastructure
metadata.definition: The indicator is represented as a percentage ratio of the volume of retained pollution and the volume of produced pollution i.e. retained and emitted.
metadata.methodology: The value of this indicator is between 0 and 100%. The closer to 100% is the value, the bigger is the protection potential of a particular source of pollution.Data on the volume of contained or neutralized particulate pollution illustrate volume of pollution reduced in devices designed for protection of atmosphere, installed at plants of significant nuisance to air quality. Efficiency of cleaning devices determined as the level of pollution reduction is the characteristic figure for equipment and shows what percentage of the total pollution has been retained in the device.The actual effectiveness of the device operation is equal to or lower than the assumed operating effectiveness. This is because the device operation conditions (e.g. chemical composition of the flowing particulates and gases, grain composition of particulates, flow of gas, temperature, humidity and pressure of gas and other) as well as its availability.Plants of significant nuisance to air quality are the so-called point sources of pollution emission and  include all organizational units specified by the Minister of Environment Protection and Natural Resources on the basis of the amount of fees paid in 1986 for an annual emission of air pollutants according to rates specified in the Regulation of the Council of Ministers of 13 January 1986 on fees for economic use of the environment and introduction of changes (Journal of Laws No. 7 item 40, with later amendments). Thus, a fixed set of reporting units (plants) is maintained annually, which enables to preserve continuity and comparability of research results. The set may be enlarged only in particular cases e.g. newly launched entities or expanded entities of a high threshold pollution emission.
